_comment: Machine-readable catalogue for the gmist house framework (deck-base.css). One source for the grouped/described `.` picker, the HelpPanel axes explainer, and the generated step utilities (scale, position). Phase 3 of plans/class-system.md: a build script generates the stepped rules in deck-base.css and the README catalogue from this file. Until that build exists, keep this in step with deck-base.css by hand.

component.desc: What the element is. Reads the global knobs; combine with a colour.

classes.flare: Animated inline highlight (flares in, resolves to its colour)
classes.hl: Static inline highlight (same look, no animation)
classes.panel: Padded box with an accent left border
classes.cards: Responsive grid of boxes (+ .cols-2/3/4); list items become cards
classes.card: A box inside a .cards grid (a standalone box is .bg-<colour>)
classes.bignums: List of big figures: start each item with the figure in **bold**
classes.rectangle: Outline rectangle (label inside; recolour the outline, .bg-<colour> to fill, scale + place it)
classes.circle: Outline circle (label inside; recolour the outline, .bg-<colour> to fill, scale + place it)
classes.oval: Outline oval (label inside; recolour the outline, .bg-<colour> to fill, scale + place it)
classes.callout: Alias: a neutral .panel for a noted aside
classes.brand: Causal Map logo pinned top-left (the logo's only home)
classes.caption: Plain small caption under an image (no background)

colour.desc: The element's text/accent hue (and the matching pale tint any box on the same element fills with). Use .bg-<colour> and .border-<colour> to override fill and border independently. Sets --hue, --hue-bright, --hue-pale, --hue-deep, --on.

fill.desc: Override only the background, independent of the text colour: .teal .bg-pink is teal text on a pale-pink box. Pale by default; add .solid for a strong fill.

border.desc: Draw a border in that colour: .border-teal on any box gives a teal edge. A panel keeps its left accent and a shape its outline, just taking the colour.

theme.desc: The active theme's own palette, so a box or word matches the deck. page = slide background, ink = body text, accent / accent-2 = the theme's accents. Works as text, .bg- fill and .border-.

classes.bg-page: Theme: fill with the slide background
classes.bg-ink: Theme: fill with the body-text colour
classes.bg-accent: Theme: fill with the primary accent
classes.bg-accent-2: Theme: fill with the secondary accent
classes.border-page: Theme: border in the slide background
classes.border-ink: Theme: border in the body-text colour
classes.border-accent: Theme: border in the primary accent
classes.border-accent-2: Theme: border in the secondary accent

shade.desc: Which version of the hue a component fills with.

scale.desc: Uniformly shrink or grow everything inside and reflow. .scale-50 = half, .scale-300 = triple; or .scale with --scale for any amount.

opacity.desc: Make a box, image or text translucent. .opacity-90 = 90% opaque.

fade.desc: Make only the BACKGROUND translucent (text stays opaque), for a box over an image. .fade-70 = 70% opaque fill. Pair with a .bg-/.light/.dark fill; use .opacity-* to fade the whole element instead.

order.desc: Bring later items in one after another. On its own a cascade class fades the element in on its delay (it arrives from nothing); add .flare to colour it in instead.

timing.desc: Sets the global --dur / --ease knobs the animations read.

align.desc: Alignment: horizontal (text) and vertical (a slide's content, or columns in a row).

place.desc: Float a block within the slide. Giving any coordinate (a .top-/.left- utility or a top=/left= attribute) already floats it, so .place is optional and only an explicit opt-in.

classes.place: position:absolute (optional; coordinates float a block on their own)

generated.note: Class name drops the % sign: .top-25 means top:25%.

width.desc: Fixed width for a box/panel/card/image, as a percent of the container.

height.desc: Fixed height for a box/panel/card/image, in pixels.

external._comment: Reveal.js / Quarto authoring classes that are NOT in this framework CSS. gmist keeps these in cm-classes.ts BUILTIN_CLASSES so the picker still offers them; do not duplicate their appearance here.
